rag_rules: Assume statements must be placed in component type annexes, not implementation annexes
AGREE contracts are scoped to component types unless bindings require implementation-level assignments
generic_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to implementation-level references in type annexes should be resolved by moving valid bindings to implementation annexes
AGREE scalar types must use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or undefined type names
Only visible AADL features or declared helpers should be referenced in AGREE contracts to prevent 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for assignments and bindings
anti_patterns: Placing assume statements in component implementation annexes will cause validation errors
Using undefined or package-qualified type names in AGREE const or eq declarations will result in resolution errors
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
